title: Say what you want; famous games are usually fun
overview: Ace gamer Fumiya Tomozaki has given up on the “garbage game” of life, until a classmate offers to teach him the rules of this ultimate god-tier game.

title: It feels awesome to gain a bunch of levels after a battle
overview: Hinami tasks Tomozaki with starting and joining conversations; after his first few attempts fall flat, he comes away with some astute observations.

title: When a girl’s your first friend, life feels like a date for a while
overview: With Hinami’s help, Tomozaki gets a crash course in dressing well and talking to girls, getting unexpectedly close to one classmate in particular.

title: Sometimes you conquer a dungeon only to find a strong boss back in your village
overview: Tomozaki begins tutoring Izumi in his favorite game, comes clean to Kikuchi, and gets roped into playing against the vengeful Nakamura after school.

title: The characters who become your friends after you clear a hard event usually have high stats
overview: Tomozaki makes a new friend, then goes on a shopping trip for Nakamura’s birthday—of all things. Naturally, Hinami turns it into a teaching moment.

title: Once you start speedrunning the minigames, you seriously can’t stop
overview: The student council election is approaching, and Tomozaki joins Mimimi’s campaign. Turns out they both have their reasons for wanting to beat Hinami.

title: When a mentor character becomes a boss, they’ll push you to the edge
overview: Tomozaki brings his gamer’s cunning to Mimimi’s campaign speech—but Hinami’s a gamer, too. Tama tells Tomozaki how she and Mimimi became friends.

title: There are some problems a bottom-tier character can’t fix alone
overview: Mimimi’s at her breaking point. A deeper dive into her past reveals why she’s so driven to beat Hinami, while Hinami’s motivation remains a mystery.

title: When you go back to the starter town with the full party, new stuff tends to happen
overview: Tomozaki rehearses with Hinami to prepare for his movie date with Kikuchi and is invited to an overnight barbecue with his popular normie classmates.

title: Multiplayer games have their own special appeal
overview: Tomozaki gets a taste of the traditional overnight normie camp activities he’d always shunned as the group plots to get Izumi and Nakamura together.

title: A single choice can change everything
overview: An overheard conversation between Mizusawa and Hinami gives Tomozaki a different perspective on his private lessons in conquering the game of life.

title: Equipment for girls has special effects
overview: A talk with Kikuchi helps Tomozaki realize what he really wants, and a confrontation with Hinami sets the course for the future of their relationship.

title: The best games make reconnaissance fun
overview: As the second term begins, Tomozaki's first task (courtesy of Hinano) is getting popular girl Erika Konno motivated for the school sports tournament.

title: A happy ending doesn't mean this game is over
overview: Erika Konno starts lashing out at her classmates after the school's sports festival, and opinions are deeply divided on the best way to shut her down.

title: Battles go better when you're fighting alongside someone whose signature move is the opposite of yours
overview: Tomozaki enlists the help of friends to help Tama improve and boost the mood at school, but Aoi Hinami is vehemently opposed to Tama changing at all.

title: Villagers have their own way of life
overview: Team Tomozaki continues to coach Tama in becoming more integrated with the class. Tomozaki brings in a special pinch-hitter with wise words to share.

title: If you keep upgrading your initial equipment, it'll usually become your most powerful weapon
overview: Simmering classroom unrest over Konno and Tama reaches a boiling point as Aoi steps in, revealing a side of herself that Tomozaki didn't want to see.

title: Everyone has different expectations about a big event
overview: Tomozaki confronts Hinami over her handling of the Konno affair. He's tasked with making a good impression on his classmates--plus a big decision.

title: Important items are usually lying around in enchanted forests
overview: It's up to Tomozaki to find a suitable play to perform at the culture festival, all while trying to check more of Hinami's tasks off his Pinsta list.

title: Sometimes the main character can't enter the village of another species on his own
overview: Tomozaki and Mizusawa scope out a girls' school festival (and its girls). Tomozaki has a deep discussion with Fuka the author. Mimimi drops a bomb.

title: If you don't make up your mind, the story won't advance
overview: Things get awkward around Mimimi. Tomozaki finally chooses the two girls he'd like to pursue, and Hinami gives him more tasks in pursuit of his goals.

title: The patterns carved into the stone tablet are connected to the mysteries of the world
overview: Roles are assigned in the class play, and Kikuchi goes in a new direction while revising her script; as research, she wants to know more about Hinami.

title: Even fairies feel lonely when they're away from their spring by themselves
overview: As rehearsals begin and Kikuchi polishes her script, she reveals her philosophy to a concerned Tomozaki. Mimimi and Tomozaki work on their comedy.

title: Sometimes the ancient tome in the first town is the key to the final dungeon
overview: Tomozaki tries to show Kikuchi the issues with her world view. Mimimi and Tomozaki perform their comedy routine. The curtain rises on the class play.

title: You're sure to find what you want on the other side of the magic door
overview: The class play goes off without a hitch, but with a surprise twist. Afterward, Tomozaki seeks out Kikuchi for a conversation about their relationship.
